---
title: "Session 2: AI for Research and Advanced Teaching"
subtitle: "April 27, 2026 — AI Bootcamp for UVM Economics"
---

## April 27, 11:00--12:30, Old Mill A500

Last session was single prompts and one-off tasks. This session is about chaining steps into workflows — research pipelines, slide creation, teaching applications, and automation.

### Slides

| Deck | Format |
|------|--------|
| Slide Creation, Teaching, and Research Workflows (Beam) | [HTML](slides/s2-part1.html){target="_blank"} / [PDF](slides/s2-part1.pdf) |
| Web Scraping + Automated Grading (Aslim) | [HTML](slides/s2-part2.html){target="_blank"} / [PDF](slides/s2-part2.pdf) |

### What we'll cover

- How to chain AI steps into a documented workflow (plan, execute, review, clear, repeat)
- Creating slides from rough notes
- Teaching applications: assignments, rubrics, study guides
- Research pipeline: raw CPS data to analysis plan to Stata code to report
- Web scraping and automated grading (Erkmen)

### What to try next

- **Teaching**: Draft a new assignment, create practice problems, or build a study guide
- **Research**: Try the data exploration → analysis plan workflow on your own data
- **Writing**: Use Claude Code to revise a draft, check references, or restructure a section

### More things to try

These didn't make it into the live session but are worth exploring on your own:

**Writing and admin**

- Rewrite an email to be shorter, warmer, and clearer
- Summarize meeting notes into decisions, action items, and open questions
- Extract deadlines from a document into a table
- Turn rough notes into a clean memo

**Project management**

- Make a step-by-step plan before starting a task
- Keep a running list of decisions, open questions, and next steps
- Summarize project state so you can reopen it later
- Create a reusable prompt for a recurring task

**Compare outputs**

- Ask for two approaches to the same task
- Compare outputs and use disagreement as a signal to look more carefully
- Send one version to stress-test the other

### Resources

- [Getting Set Up](prep.qmd) — installation and cost guide
- [Outside Resources](resources.qmd) — tools, guides, and examples
- [Claude Code docs](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code)
- [UVM Microsoft Copilot](https://go.uvm.edu/copilot) (free for all UVM)

### The subscription question

AI subscriptions (Claude Pro, ChatGPT Plus) cost ~$20/month. UVM provides Microsoft Copilot for free. PDF funds may cover subscriptions — talk to your chair.
